title: #WelcomeAfghans: Follow the journey - Canada.ca
content:
## #WelcomeAfghans: Follow the journey
The Government of Canada is committed to welcoming at least 40,000 Afghan refugees to Canada. These are some of the photos, stories and announcements from this journey.
Photos of previous Afghan refugee arrivals are available in Dropbox for use by media .
Afghan journalist finds new ways to fight against oppression and help other newcomers
The unwavering voice of Afghan women
Musician finding new ways to use her voice
Educator building a brighter future for himself and others in Canada
Activist and teacher helping Afghan women settle in Canada
Journalist working to promote education for Afghan children
Afghan-Canadian journalist and a passionate learner advocates for Afghanistan’s most vulnerable people
Comedian becomes the uncensored Afghan voice against injustice
Afghan surgeon finds peace through painting

### The people behind the initiative
Meet a self-taught Farsi speaker who used his talent to talk Afghan refugees through their escape to safety
Welcoming Afghan newcomers as they disembark on Canadian soil
Canada meets commitment to welcome 40,000 vulnerable Afghans
Canada welcomes Afghans through new humanitarian partnership
Canada marks significant milestone, welcoming over 30,000 vulnerable Afghans
Minister Fraser welcomes latest charter flight of Afghan refugees from Pakistan - Canada.ca
Canada marks 20,000 Afghan arrivals with help from partners at home and abroad
Statement by Ministers Joly, Fraser, Sajjan and Anand one year after fall of Afghanistan
Afghan newcomers to celebrate first Canada Day
School programs help Afghan children thrive in Canada
Afghan newcomers continue to arrive in Canada
Canada marks 15,000 arrivals of Afghan refugees
Canada celebrates Pride Month by helping Afghan LGBTQ2 individuals
Settlement organizations help Afghan refugees adjust to new life in Canada
Businesses offer jobs for Afghan newcomers
Afghan journalists find freedom of expression in Canada
Canadians continue to support Afghan refugees across the country
Canada marks 10,000 arrivals of Afghan refugees
Service provider helps connect Afghan newcomers to meaningful employment
Minister Fraser meets with Afghan newcomers  and settlement services organizations in Alberta
Afghan women and girls make their mark in Canada
Canadian educational institutions support Afghan newcomers
Canadians continue to welcome Afghan refugees in their communities
Canada continues to welcome more Afghan refugees
Canada welcomes over 200 Afghan refugees to Vancouver
Afghan human rights defenders arrive in Canada
Notice – Application process for pathway to permanent residence for extended families of Afghan interpreters now open
Minister Fraser welcomes home Afghan refugees arriving today
First charter flight of privately sponsored Afghan refugees  arrives in Canada
Notice – Canada announces new measures for extended families of Afghan interpreters
Notice – Changes to the pre-removal risk assessment for Afghanistan
More than 3,000 Evacuees Arrive in Canada from Afghanistan
Canada reaches agreement with U.S. to support refugees’ travel to safety
Notice – Support for Afghan citizens with travel authorizations to leave Afghanistan
Canada expands resettlement program to bring more Afghans to safety
Canada welcomes first Afghan refugees resettled through the special immigration program
Government of Canada offers refuge to Afghans who assisted Canada
